data.narrative:
Type One Energy is a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised over $100M and carries a current post-money valuation of $200M+. The valuation universe includes 11 comparable companies across the nuclear sector: 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private players including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis applies directly to this dataset. Using Type One's $200M last post-money valuation as the base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage progression, this method yields an implied valuation of $380M. The peer-median approach was not applicable; although 7 same-sector comparables exist, the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, and valuation tier) returned insufficient datapoints to establish a reliable median, and a broader all-sector median would introduce misleading heterogeneity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $323M to $437M, with $380M as the median. This single-method result carries material uncertainty. The primary limitation is reliance on stage-progression mechanics alone, without corroboration from peer trading or transaction multiples. Key unknowns include capital efficiency relative to nuclear sector peers, technology differentiation, regulatory pathway maturity, and path to commercialization—factors that typically drive wide valuation dispersion in frontier energy. Additional comparable transaction or trading data would substantially narrow this range.
